payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives after the action actually succeeds. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

payload.message:
lelele，我找到一个很像真因的回归点了，而且它**正好是 `11.6.2` 之后改过的**。

### 我现在的判断
`ar/event` 已经到了，本地 action 链本身大概率也没断。  
**真正坏掉的，更像是“弹窗要展示时，拿错了宿主窗口/宿主 VC”。**

关键差异在 [WBAppManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBAppManager.m)：

#### `11.6.2` 里
`getUIApplicationRootViewController` 是直接：

```objc
return UIApplication.sharedApplication.keyWindow.rootViewController;
```

#### 现在
改成了：

```objc
for (UIWindowScene *scene in [UIApplication sharedApplication].connectedScenes) {
    if (scene.activationState == UISceneActivationStateForegroundActive) {
        window = scene.windows.firstObject;
        break;
    }
}
return window.rootViewController;
```

这里最大的问题不是 `scene`，而是 **`scene.windows.firstObject` 不可靠**。  
它不一定是当前正在显示内容的那个 window，更不一定是 key window。

而你这条弹窗链最终会走到 [WBPopupManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBPopupManager.m)：

```objc
UIViewController *rootVC = [WBAppManager getUIApplicationRootViewController];
UIViewController *hostVC = rootVC.presentedViewController ?: rootVC;
[hostVC presentViewController:popupVC animated:false completion:nil];
```

也就是说：

- 现在先拿了一个可能不对的 `rootVC`
- 然后又只看了一层 `presentedViewController`
- 在 vapp 运行态下，这就很容易挂到错误宿主上
- 结果就是：**服务器让你弹，代码也走到了，但 UI 没出来**
- UI 没出来，后面的 `Exit` 操作自然也不会触发
- 所以你看到的是“没有展示，也没有强退”

### 为什么这和你说的现象对得上
因为我对比了 `v11.6.2`：

- `ActionRequest`
- `WBDefaultActionFactory`
- `WBLinkActionModel`
- `WBPopupManager`
- `WBPopupContentViewController`
- `WBPopUpAlertController`

这些核心 action / popup 代码，和 `11.6.2` 基本没变。

**反而是 `WBAppManager` 里拿 rootVC 的方式变了。**

所以现在最像的是：
不是弹窗逻辑变了，而是**弹窗还在老地方弹，但宿主窗口选错了**。

---

## 改法建议
我建议按最小修复做两步：

1. 修 `getUIApplicationRootViewController`
   - 不要拿 `scene.windows.firstObject`
   - 改成优先找当前 scene 里的 `isKeyWindow`
   - 找不到再 fallback 到 `UIApplication.sharedApplication.keyWindow`

2. 修 `WBPopupManager showPopup`
   - 不要只用 `rootVC.presentedViewController ?: rootVC`
   - 直接改成用 `getUIApplicationCurrentViewController`
   - 这样就算层级更深，也能挂到当前最上层

---

## 这次我不建议先改“自动强退”
因为从代码看，现在“强退”本来就是依赖弹窗按钮点击的。  
你这次先把**弹窗展示恢复**，大概率“点击后退出”也会一起恢复。

如果你的业务预期是“弹窗一出现或倒计时结束就自动退出”，那是第二层需求，得单独补逻辑，不是这次回归的核心点。

---

## 我准备这么改
如果你同意，我就改这两个文件：

- [WBAppManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBAppManager.m)
- [WBPopupManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBPopupManager.m)

先把行为尽量拉回 `11.6.2` 那种稳定状态，再看要不要补“自动退出”。

你点头我就动。

所以现在最像的是：
不是弹窗逻辑变了，而是**弹窗还在老地方弹，但宿主窗口选错了**。

---

## 改法建议
我建议按最小修复做两步：

1. 修 `getUIApplicationRootViewController`
   - 不要拿 `scene.windows.firstObject`
   - 改成优先找当前 scene 里的 `isKeyWindow`
   - 找不到再 fallback 到 `UIApplication.sharedApplication.keyWindow`

2. 修 `WBPopupManager showPopup`
   - 不要只用 `rootVC.presentedViewController ?: rootVC`
   - 直接改成用 `getUIApplicationCurrentViewController`
   - 这样就算层级更深，也能挂到当前最上层

---

## 这次我不建议先改“自动强退”
因为从代码看，现在“强退”本来就是依赖弹窗按钮点击的。  
你这次先把**弹窗展示恢复**，大概率“点击后退出”也会一起恢复。

如果你的业务预期是“弹窗一出现或倒计时结束就自动退出”，那是第二层需求，得单独补逻辑，不是这次回归的核心点。

---

## 我准备这么改
如果你同意，我就改这两个文件：

- [WBAppManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBAppManager.m)
- [WBPopupManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBPopupManager.m)

先把行为尽量拉回 `11.6.2` 那种稳定状态，再看要不要补“自动退出”。

payload.last_agent_message:
lelele，我找到一个很像真因的回归点了，而且它**正好是 `11.6.2` 之后改过的**。

### 我现在的判断
`ar/event` 已经到了，本地 action 链本身大概率也没断。  
**真正坏掉的，更像是“弹窗要展示时，拿错了宿主窗口/宿主 VC”。**

关键差异在 [WBAppManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBAppManager.m)：

#### `11.6.2` 里
`getUIApplicationRootViewController` 是直接：

```objc
return UIApplication.sharedApplication.keyWindow.rootViewController;
```

#### 现在
改成了：

```objc
for (UIWindowScene *scene in [UIApplication sharedApplication].connectedScenes) {
    if (scene.activationState == UISceneActivationStateForegroundActive) {
        window = scene.windows.firstObject;
        break;
    }
}
return window.rootViewController;
```

这里最大的问题不是 `scene`，而是 **`scene.windows.firstObject` 不可靠**。  
它不一定是当前正在显示内容的那个 window，更不一定是 key window。

而你这条弹窗链最终会走到 [WBPopupManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBPopupManager.m)：

```objc
UIViewController *rootVC = [WBAppManager getUIApplicationRootViewController];
UIViewController *hostVC = rootVC.presentedViewController ?: rootVC;
[hostVC presentViewController:popupVC animated:false completion:nil];
```

也就是说：

- 现在先拿了一个可能不对的 `rootVC`
- 然后又只看了一层 `presentedViewController`
- 在 vapp 运行态下，这就很容易挂到错误宿主上
- 结果就是：**服务器让你弹，代码也走到了，但 UI 没出来**
- UI 没出来，后面的 `Exit` 操作自然也不会触发
- 所以你看到的是“没有展示，也没有强退”

### 为什么这和你说的现象对得上
因为我对比了 `v11.6.2`：

- `ActionRequest`
- `WBDefaultActionFactory`
- `WBLinkActionModel`
- `WBPopupManager`
- `WBPopupContentViewController`
- `WBPopUpAlertController`

这些核心 action / popup 代码，和 `11.6.2` 基本没变。

**反而是 `WBAppManager` 里拿 rootVC 的方式变了。**

所以现在最像的是：
不是弹窗逻辑变了，而是**弹窗还在老地方弹，但宿主窗口选错了**。

---

## 改法建议
我建议按最小修复做两步：

1. 修 `getUIApplicationRootViewController`
   - 不要拿 `scene.windows.firstObject`
   - 改成优先找当前 scene 里的 `isKeyWindow`
   - 找不到再 fallback 到 `UIApplication.sharedApplication.keyWindow`

2. 修 `WBPopupManager showPopup`
   - 不要只用 `rootVC.presentedViewController ?: rootVC`
   - 直接改成用 `getUIApplicationCurrentViewController`
   - 这样就算层级更深，也能挂到当前最上层

---

## 这次我不建议先改“自动强退”
因为从代码看，现在“强退”本来就是依赖弹窗按钮点击的。

# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives after the action actually succeeds. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

content.text: action链的入口在哪

payload.message: action链的入口在哪

lelele，`action` 链入口分两层，你这个场景里最关键的是这条：

### 1. `ar/event` 请求入口
从事件上报进来，入口在  
[WBActionRequestManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/WBActionRequestManager.m)

常走的是这两个方法：

- `- requestReportEventWithType:defaultHandler:`
- `- requestReportEventWithType:data:defaultHandler:`

它们内部会调到  
[WBNetworkManager+ActionRequest.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/WBNetworkManager+ActionRequest.m)

这里真正发请求的是：

- `- requestReportEventWithType:callback:`
- `- requestReportEventWithType:data:callback:`

最后打到服务端接口：

```objc
[self requestActionRequestInterfaceWithURI:@"ar/event" params:params callback:callback];
```

---

### 2. 服务端回包后的 action 执行入口
服务端返回后，先在  
[WBNetworkManager+ActionRequest.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/WBNetworkManager+ActionRequest.m)

这里把 json 转成 action model：

```objc
WBBaseActionModel *actionModel = [[WBDefaultActionFactory sharedFactory] buildActionWithDict:json sourceInterface:uri];
```

然后回到  
[WBActionRequestManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/WBActionRequestManager.m)

统一走这里：

```objc
- (void)handleActionModel:(WBBaseActionModel *)actionModel error:(NSError *)error defaultHandler:(WBActionRequestDefaultHandler)defaultHandler
```

真正执行 action 的一脚是：

```objc
[actionModel executeWithDefaultHandler:defaultHandler];
```

---

## 这条链完整顺序
你这个“vapp 使用时长到了，服务端通过 `ar/event` 下发弹窗/退出”对应链路大概是：

1. 某个事件触发  
   比如 heartbeat 里这几个：
   [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m)
   的
   - `WBActionRequestEventTypeVappRunningInForeground`
   - `WBActionRequestEventTypeVappRunningInBackground`

2. 调到  
   [WBActionRequestManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/WBActionRequestManager.m)

3. 发 `ar/event`  
   [WBNetworkManager+ActionRequest.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/WBNetworkManager+ActionRequest.m)

4. 回包进 factory  
   [WBDefaultActionFactory.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/ActionFactory/Impl/WBDefaultActionFactory.m)

5. 执行 model  
   [WBActionRequestManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/WBActionRequestManager.m)

6. 如果是 link action，就进 route  
   [WBLinkActionModel.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/ActionTypes/Impl/WBLinkActionModel.m)

7. 再由  
   [WBRouteManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteManager.m)  
   和  
   [WBRouteActionManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteActionManager.m)  
   去做 popup / exit / vapp 等具体动作

---

## 一句话定位
如果你要打断点，我建议从这 4 个点开始最值钱：

1. [WBActionRequestManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/WBActionRequestManager.m) 的 `requestReportEventWithType`
2. [WBNetworkManager+ActionRequest.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/WBNetworkManager+ActionRequest.m) 的 `requestActionRequestInterfaceWithURI:@"ar/event"`
3. [WBActionRequestManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/WBActionRequestManager.m) 的 `handleActionModel`
4. [WBBaseActionModel 子类](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/ActionTypes/Impl/WBLinkActionModel.m) 的 `executeWithDefaultHandler`

如果你愿意，我下一条可以直接给你画一张“你这个 vapp 超时弹窗”的**实际调用链**，把具体函数名串成一条线。

常走的是这两个方法：

- `- requestReportEventWithType:defaultHandler:`
- `- requestReportEventWithType:data:defaultHandler:`

它们内部会调到  
[WBNetworkManager+ActionRequest.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/WBNetworkManager+ActionRequest.m)

这里真正发请求的是：

- `- requestReportEventWithType:callback:`
- `- requestReportEventWithType:data:callback:`

最后打到服务端接口：

```objc
[self requestActionRequestInterfaceWithURI:@"ar/event" params:params callback:callback];
```

---

### 2. 服务端回包后的 action 执行入口
服务端返回后，先在  
[WBNetworkManager+ActionRequest.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/WBNetworkManager+ActionRequest.m)

这里把 json 转成 action model：

```objc
WBBaseActionModel *actionModel = [[WBDefaultActionFactory sharedFactory] buildActionWithDict:json sourceInterface:uri];
```

然后回到  
[WBActionRequestManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/WBActionRequestManager.m)

统一走这里：

```objc
- (void)handleActionModel:(WBBaseActionModel *)actionModel error:(NSError *)error defaultHandler:(WBActionRequestDefaultHandler)defaultHandler
```

真正执行 action 的一脚是：

```objc
[actionModel executeWithDefaultHandler:defaultHandler];
```

---

## 这条链完整顺序
你这个“vapp 使用时长到了，服务端通过 `ar/event` 下发弹窗/退出”对应链路大概是：

1. 某个事件触发  
   比如 heartbeat 里这几个：
   [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m)
   的
   - `WBActionRequestEventTypeVappRunningInForeground`
   - `WBActionRequestEventTypeVappRunningInBackground`

2. 调到  
   [WBActionRequestManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/WBActionRequestManager.m)

3. 发 `ar/event`  
   [WBNetworkManager+ActionRequest.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/WBNetworkManager+ActionRequest.m)

4. 回包进 factory  
   [WBDefaultActionFactory.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/ActionFactory/Impl/WBDefaultActionFactory.m)

5. 执行 model  
   [WBActionRequestManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/WBActionRequestManager.m)

6. 如果是 link action，就进 route  
   [WBLinkActionModel.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/ActionTypes/Impl/WBLinkActionModel.m)

7. 再由  
   [WBRouteManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteManager.m)  
   和  
   [WBRouteActionManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteActionManager.m)  
   去做 popup / exit / vapp 等具体动作

---

## 一句话定位
如果你要打断点，我建议从这 4 个点开始最值钱：

1. [WBActionRequestManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/WBActionRequestManager.m) 的 `requestReportEventWithType`
2. [WBNetworkManager+ActionRequest.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/WBNetworkManager+ActionRequest.m) 的 `requestActionRequestInterfaceWithURI:@"ar/event"`
3. [WBActionRequestManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/WBActionRequestManager.m) 的 `handleActionModel`
4. [WBBaseActionModel 子类](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/ActionTypes/Impl/WBLinkActionModel.m) 的 `executeWithDefaultHandler`

如果你愿意，我下一条可以直接给你画一张“你这个 vapp 超时弹窗”的**实际调用链**，把具体函数名串成一条线。

# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives after the action actually succeeds. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

payload.message:
lelele，这段 `deliver` 往下不是“直接一路顺着调下去”，而是靠 `handler(firstRouter)` 一层一层把 router 往后推。

我按你这个项目里的实际链路给你拆一下。

## 这段代码接下来怎么走

你现在停在这里：

```objc
handler(firstRouter);
```

真正下一步会进 `handler` 这个 block 里，然后走这几件事：

1. 把 `firstRouter` 丢进去
2. 在 `currentQueue` 上排一个 block
3. 这个 block 里先判断：
   - `router == nil`
   - 或者 `_checkRecursiveBounds == true`
4. 如果都不是，就执行：
   - `weakSelf.currentRouter = router;`
   - `[weakSelf _routeHandle:router handler:handler queue:currentQueue];`

也就是说，**核心不是 `deliver` 本身**，而是后面的 `_routeHandle:router handler:queue:`。

---

## 你这个项目里，通常会怎么递进

结合 [WBRouteManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteManager.m) 和 [WBRouteActionManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteActionManager.m)，大概是这么走：

### 如果 URL 是 `webox:///action/popup?...`

### 第 1 跳
`firstRouter` 一般是 `WBRouteManager`

在 [WBRouteManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteManager.m) 里它注册了两个入口：

- `page`
- `action`

所以 `_routeHandle` 处理 `WBRouteManager` 时，会根据 path 的当前段去匹配。

如果当前段是 `action`，就会走这里：

```objc
[route name:@"action" handle:^(NSDictionary * _Nullable userInfo, JUNRouterNextHandler  _Nonnull next) {
    WBRouteActionManager *actionManager = [WBRouteActionManager sharedManager];
    ...
    next(actionManager);
}];
```

这一步执行完，`next(actionManager)` 会再次回到你刚才那个 `handler(router)`。

### 第 2 跳
这次 `router = WBRouteActionManager`

然后 `_routeHandle` 再处理下一段 path，比如 `popup`。

在 [WBRouteActionManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteActionManager.m) 里会命中：

```objc
[route name:@"popup" handle:^(NSDictionary * _Nullable userInfo, JUNRouterNextHandler  _Nonnull next) {
    NSDictionary *richMessagesData = [userInfo[@"data"] jsonValueDecoded];
    ...
    [self handleRichMessages:richMessagesData];
    ...
    next(nil);
}];
```

这一步如果正常，就会进：

- `handleRichMessages:`
- `showPopupForStyle:withUserInfo:`
- `WBPopupManager showPopup`

### 第 3 跳
`next(nil)`

当 route block 里 `next(nil)` 后，再回到 `handler(nil)`。

这时候会走到结束分支：

```objc
if (router == nil || [weakSelf _checkRecursiveBounds] == true) {
    ...
    completionHandler(weakSelf.currentRouter);
    ...
    weakSelf.isIdle = true;
    return;
}
```

所以这条 route 就结束了。

---

## 对你这个超时弹窗场景，完整链路应该是

1. [WBLinkActionModel.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/ActionTypes/Impl/WBLinkActionModel.m)  
   `executeWithDefaultHandler`

2. [WBRouteManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteManager.m)  
   `performRoute:sourceInterface:`

3. `routeExpress deliver:url toFirstRouter:self ...`

4. `handler(firstRouter)`  
   第一跳到 `WBRouteManager`

5. `_routeHandle(WBRouteManager, ...)`  
   命中 `action`

6. `next([WBRouteActionManager sharedManager])`

7. `handler(actionManager)`  
   第二跳到 `WBRouteActionManager`

8. `_routeHandle(WBRouteActionManager, ...)`  
   命中 `popup` 或 `exit` 或 `vapp`

9. 如果是 `popup`  
   进 [WBRouteActionManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteActionManager.m) 的 `handleRichMessages`

10. 再进 [WBPopupManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBPopupManager.m) 的 `showPopup`

11. 用户点按钮后  
   进 [WBPopupManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBPopupManager.m) 的 `operationClicked`

12. 如果按钮类型是 `WBRichMessageLinkTypeExit`  
   才会真的 `exit(0)`

---

## 你现在最该打的断点

如果你想确认“执行到这应该怎么走”，我建议直接打这 5 个点，最省时间：

1. [WBRouteManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteManager.m) 里 `name:@"action"` 的 block
2. [WBRouteActionManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteActionManager.m) 里 `name:@"popup"` 的 block
3. [WBRouteActionManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteActionManager.m) 里 `handleRichMessages:`
4. [WBPopupManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBPopupManager.m) 里 `showPopup`
5. [WBPopupManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBPopupManager.m) 里 `operationClicked:style:`

---

## 你在 `deliver` 这里该看什么变量

停在这里时，最有价值的是看这几个值：

- `url.absoluteString`
- `url.path`
- `firstRouter`
- `weakSelf.cursor`
- `weakSelf.currentRouter`

如果是你这个弹窗场景，正常应该看到：

- `firstRouter` 是 `WBRouteManager`
- `url.path` 像 `/action/popup`
- 然后下一跳应该命中 `WBRouteManager` 的 `action`
- 再下一跳命中 `WBRouteActionManager` 的 `popup`

如果 `deliver` 走了，但后面没进 `popup`，那就说明问题在：

- URL 不对
- path 解析不对
- `_routeHandle` 没匹配上当前段

如果进了 `popup`，但没进 `showPopup`，那就看：

- `userInfo[@"data"]`
- `richMessagesData`
- `style`
- `handleRichMessages:` 有没有提前 return

如果进了 `showPopup` 但界面没出来，那就回到我们前面说的 presenter 问题了。

如果你要，我下一条可以直接按你这个“vapp 超时 -> ar/event -> route -> popup”的链路，给你标出**每一步该看什么值**。

```objc
[route name:@"popup" handle:^(NSDictionary * _Nullable userInfo, JUNRouterNextHandler  _Nonnull next) {
    NSDictionary *richMessagesData = [userInfo[@"data"] jsonValueDecoded];
    ...
    [self handleRichMessages:richMessagesData];
    ...
    next(nil);
}];
```

这一步如果正常，就会进：

- `handleRichMessages:`
- `showPopupForStyle:withUserInfo:`
- `WBPopupManager showPopup`

### 第 3 跳
`next(nil)`

当 route block 里 `next(nil)` 后，再回到 `handler(nil)`。

这时候会走到结束分支：

```objc
if (router == nil || [weakSelf _checkRecursiveBounds] == true) {
    ...
    completionHandler(weakSelf.currentRouter);
    ...
    weakSelf.isIdle = true;
    return;
}
```

所以这条 route 就结束了。

---

## 对你这个超时弹窗场景，完整链路应该是

1. [WBLinkActionModel.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/ActionTypes/Impl/WBLinkActionModel.m)  
   `executeWithDefaultHandler`

2. [WBRouteManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteManager.m)  
   `performRoute:sourceInterface:`

3. `routeExpress deliver:url toFirstRouter:self ...`

4. `handler(firstRouter)`  
   第一跳到 `WBRouteManager`

5. `_routeHandle(WBRouteManager, ...)`  
   命中 `action`

6. `next([WBRouteActionManager sharedManager])`

7. `handler(actionManager)`  
   第二跳到 `WBRouteActionManager`

8. `_routeHandle(WBRouteActionManager, ...)`  
   命中 `popup` 或 `exit` 或 `vapp`

9. 如果是 `popup`  
   进 [WBRouteActionManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteActionManager.m) 的 `handleRichMessages`

10. 再进 [WBPopupManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBPopupManager.m) 的 `showPopup`

11. 用户点按钮后  
   进 [WBPopupManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBPopupManager.m) 的 `operationClicked`

12. 如果按钮类型是 `WBRichMessageLinkTypeExit`  
   才会真的 `exit(0)`

---

## 你现在最该打的断点

如果你想确认“执行到这应该怎么走”，我建议直接打这 5 个点，最省时间：

1. [WBRouteManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteManager.m) 里 `name:@"action"` 的 block
2. [WBRouteActionManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteActionManager.m) 里 `name:@"popup"` 的 block
3. [WBRouteActionManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteActionManager.m) 里 `handleRichMessages:`
4. [WBPopupManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBPopupManager.m) 里 `showPopup`
5. [WBPopupManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBPopupManager.m) 里 `operationClicked:style:`

---

## 你在 `deliver` 这里该看什么变量

停在这里时，最有价值的是看这几个值：

- `url.absoluteString`
- `url.path`
- `firstRouter`
- `weakSelf.cursor`
- `weakSelf.currentRouter`

如果是你这个弹窗场景，正常应该看到：

- `firstRouter` 是 `WBRouteManager`
- `url.path` 像 `/action/popup`
- 然后下一跳应该命中 `WBRouteManager` 的 `action`
- 再下一跳命中 `WBRouteActionManager` 的 `popup`

如果 `deliver` 走了，但后面没进 `popup`，那就说明问题在：

- URL 不对
- path 解析不对
- `_routeHandle` 没匹配上当前段

如果进了 `popup`，但没进 `showPopup`，那就看：

- `userInfo[@"data"]`
- `richMessagesData`
- `style`
- `handleRichMessages:` 有没有提前 return

如果进了 `showPopup` 但界面没出来，那就回到我们前面说的 presenter 问题了。

如果你要，我下一条可以直接按你这个“vapp 超时 -> ar/event -> route -> popup”的链路，给你标出**每一步该看什么值**。

lelele，这段 `deliver` 往下不是“直接一路顺着调下去”，而是靠 `handler(firstRouter)` 一层一层把 router 往后推。

我按你这个项目里的实际链路给你拆一下。

## 这段代码接下来怎么走

你现在停在这里：

```objc
handler(firstRouter);
```

真正下一步会进 `handler` 这个 block 里，然后走这几件事：

1. 把 `firstRouter` 丢进去
2. 在 `currentQueue` 上排一个 block
3. 这个 block 里先判断：
   - `router == nil`
   - 或者 `_checkRecursiveBounds == true`
4. 如果都不是，就执行：
   - `weakSelf.currentRouter = router;`
   - `[weakSelf _routeHandle:router handler:handler queue:currentQueue];`

也就是说，**核心不是 `deliver` 本身**，而是后面的 `_routeHandle:router handler:queue:`。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives after the action actually succeeds. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

payload.message: handler(firstRouter);然后应该执行啥

lelele，`handler(firstRouter);` 之后，**立刻执行的就是 `handler` 这个 block 自己**，不是别的神秘方法。

你这段代码里已经写死了：

```objc
__block JUNRouterNextHandler handler = ^(id<JUNRouter> router) {
    [currentQueue addOperationWithBlock:^{
        if (router == nil || [weakSelf _checkRecursiveBounds] == true) {
            ...
            return;
        }
        weakSelf.currentRouter = router;
        [weakSelf _routeHandle:router handler:handler queue:currentQueue];
    }];
};
handler(firstRouter);
```

所以顺序是：

## 1. 先执行 `handler(firstRouter)`
把 `firstRouter` 传进 `handler`。

## 2. `handler` 里会往 `currentQueue` 塞一个 block
也就是这段会被调度执行：

```objc
[currentQueue addOperationWithBlock:^{
    ...
}];
```

## 3. 这个 block 执行后，正常情况会走到
```objc
weakSelf.currentRouter = router;
[weakSelf _routeHandle:router handler:handler queue:currentQueue];
```

所以你要问“然后应该执行啥”，**答案就是 `_routeHandle:router handler:queue:`**。

---

# 在你这个项目里，`firstRouter` 通常是谁
在 [WBRouteManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteManager.m) 里：

```objc
[self.routeExpress deliver:url toFirstRouter:self completion:completionHandler];
```

所以这里的 `firstRouter` 一般就是：

- `WBRouteManager`

---

# 所以实际执行链是
### 第一步
`handler(firstRouter)`

### 第二步
进 `handler` block

### 第三步
进 queue block

### 第四步
执行：

```objc
[weakSelf _routeHandle:firstRouter handler:handler queue:currentQueue];
```

### 第五步
`_routeHandle` 会调用当前 router 的 `jun_routeBuild` / `jun_routeHandle`

对你这个项目来说：

- 先处理 `WBRouteManager`
- 如果 URL 是 `/action/popup`
- 它会命中 `action`
- 然后 `next(actionManager)`

### 第六步
`next(actionManager)` 又会重新回调 `handler(actionManager)`

然后再重复一次：

- queue block
- `_routeHandle(actionManager, ...)`

再往下命中：

- `popup`
- `exit`
- `vapp`

---

# 你可以直接理解成
`handler(firstRouter);` 的作用就是：

**“从第一个 router 开始，驱动整条路由链往下走。”**

而真正每一跳干活的点是：

```objc
[weakSelf _routeHandle:router handler:handler queue:currentQueue];
```

---

# 如果你现在在调试
你下一步最该看的不是 `handler(firstRouter)` 本身，而是：

1. `firstRouter` 是不是 `WBRouteManager`
2. queue block 有没有真的执行
3. 有没有进 `_routeHandle:router handler:queue:`
4. 传进去的 `router` 是谁
5. `url.path` 是什么

---

# 一句话
`handler(firstRouter);` 之后，正常就该执行：

```objc
[weakSelf _routeHandle:firstRouter handler:handler queue:currentQueue];
```

如果你愿意，我可以下一条直接告诉你：

**当 URL 是 `webox:///action/popup?...` 时，`_routeHandle` 里应该依次命中哪几个 block。**

```objc

    [currentQueue addOperationWithBlock:^{
        if (router == nil || [weakSelf _checkRecursiveBounds] == true) {

        }
        weakSelf.currentRouter = router;
        [weakSelf _routeHandle:router handler:handler queue:currentQueue];
    }];
};
handler(firstRouter);
```

所以顺序是：

## 1. 先执行 `handler(firstRouter)`
把 `firstRouter` 传进 `handler`。

## 2. `handler` 里会往 `currentQueue` 塞一个 block
也就是这段会被调度执行：

```objc
[currentQueue addOperationWithBlock:^{
    ...
}];
```

## 3. 这个 block 执行后，正常情况会走到
```objc
weakSelf.currentRouter = router;
[weakSelf _routeHandle:router handler:handler queue:currentQueue];
```

所以你要问“然后应该执行啥”，**答案就是 `_routeHandle:router handler:queue:`**。

---

# 在你这个项目里，`firstRouter` 通常是谁
在 [WBRouteManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteManager.m) 里：

```objc
[self.routeExpress deliver:url toFirstRouter:self completion:completionHandler];
```

所以这里的 `firstRouter` 一般就是：

- `WBRouteManager`

---

# 所以实际执行链是
### 第一步
`handler(firstRouter)`

### 第二步
进 `handler` block

### 第三步
进 queue block

### 第四步
执行：

```objc
[weakSelf _routeHandle:firstRouter handler:handler queue:currentQueue];
```

### 第五步
`_routeHandle` 会调用当前 router 的 `jun_routeBuild` / `jun_routeHandle`

对你这个项目来说：

- 先处理 `WBRouteManager`
- 如果 URL 是 `/action/popup`
- 它会命中 `action`
- 然后 `next(actionManager)`

### 第六步
`next(actionManager)` 又会重新回调 `handler(actionManager)`

然后再重复一次：

- queue block
- `_routeHandle(actionManager, ...)`

再往下命中：

- `popup`
- `exit`
- `vapp`

---

# 你可以直接理解成
`handler(firstRouter);` 的作用就是：

**“从第一个 router 开始，驱动整条路由链往下走。”**

而真正每一跳干活的点是：

```objc
[weakSelf _routeHandle:router handler:handler queue:currentQueue];
```

---

# 如果你现在在调试
你下一步最该看的不是 `handler(firstRouter)` 本身，而是：

1. `firstRouter` 是不是 `WBRouteManager`
2. queue block 有没有真的执行
3. 有没有进 `_routeHandle:router handler:queue:`
4. 传进去的 `router` 是谁
5. `url.path` 是什么

---

# 一句话
`handler(firstRouter);` 之后，正常就该执行：

```objc
[weakSelf _routeHandle:firstRouter handler:handler queue:currentQueue];
```

如果你愿意，我可以下一条直接告诉你：

**当 URL 是 `webox:///action/popup?...` 时，`_routeHandle` 里应该依次命中哪几个 block。**

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.user_instructions:
# 全局协作约定（系统级）

1. 我提出的所有问题，都不要直接改代码；必须先给出方案/改动点，并与我确认后再开始修改。
2. 对我提供的设计图：忽略其中的虚线边框（不作为需要实现的 UI/布局边界）。
3. 默认使用 `shuorenhua` 的表达规则回答：优先说人话、去模板感、自然一点，减少 AI 套话和空洞连接词；但不能改变事实、结论、数据、术语和代码语义。除非我明确要求保留正式/模板化表达，否则都按这个规则执行。
const additionalDeveloperPrompt = `# 必须遵守的行为规范\n
* 必须使用中文回答\n
* 在遇到需要探索源码的任务时，拆分出关键词，进行grep搜索，一次性读取所有相关文件进行分析，读文件时如果文件代码没有超过600行，不要分段读取，一次全部读入所有内容，必须在最少的交互会话次数内完成工作\n
* 编辑文件时，如果涉及到整个文件的过多次修改。不要使用Edit工具多次修改，而必须使用Write工具一次性生成文件。生成代码时，最大限度保持现有代码，不要随意格式化代码，只修改或增加必要的部分\n
* 模仿claude的风格回复用户\n
* 生成代码时，可读性一定要好，便于人类理解，但不需要很多注释。在保证逻辑清晰，正确的基础上要做到代码精炼，用最少的代码做最多的事情，但是不能破坏可读性。\n`
* 每次回复都喊我lelele
payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives after the action actually succeeds. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

content.text: _routeHandle之后呢

payload.message: _routeHandle之后呢

lelele，`_routeHandle` 之后，就看**当前这个 router 把 `next(...)` 往哪里传**。

你可以把它理解成：

- `deliver` 负责驱动
- `handler` 负责一跳一跳往前推
- `_routeHandle` 负责“当前这个 router 到底怎么处理这段路由”

---

## 你这条链的正常顺序是这样

假设 URL 是：

```objc
webox:///action/popup?data=...
```

### 1. `handler(firstRouter)`
这里的 `firstRouter` 是 `WBRouteManager`

### 2. `_routeHandle(WBRouteManager, ...)`
这一步会去执行 `WBRouteManager` 里注册的 route 规则，也就是  
[WBRouteManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteManager.m)

它里面有：

```objc
[route name:@"action" handle:^(NSDictionary * _Nullable userInfo, JUNRouterNextHandler  _Nonnull next) {
    WBRouteActionManager *actionManager = [WBRouteActionManager sharedManager];
    ...
    next(actionManager);
}];
```

### 3. `next(actionManager)`
这句就是 `_routeHandle` 之后最关键的动作。

它不会结束，而是会**再次调用外面的 `handler`**：

```objc
handler(actionManager)
```

---

## 所以 `_routeHandle` 之后，正常应该继续执行：
```objc
next(actionManager)
```

然后又回到：

```objc
handler(actionManager)
```

然后又会走：

```objc
[weakSelf _routeHandle:actionManager handler:handler queue:currentQueue];
```

---

## 到第二跳
这时候 current router 变成 `WBRouteActionManager`

然后 `_routeHandle(WBRouteActionManager, ...)` 会继续匹配下一段路由，比如：

- `popup`
- `exit`
- `vapp`

如果是 `popup`，就会命中  
[WBRouteActionManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteActionManager.m)

```objc
[route name:@"popup" handle:^(NSDictionary * _Nullable userInfo, JUNRouterNextHandler  _Nonnull next) {
    NSDictionary *richMessagesData = [userInfo[@"data"] jsonValueDecoded];
    ...
    [self handleRichMessages:richMessagesData];
    ...
    next(nil);
}];
```

---

## 所以第二个 `_routeHandle` 之后，正常应该执行：
```objc
[self handleRichMessages:richMessagesData];
next(nil);
```

---

## 然后 `next(nil)` 会怎样
`next(nil)` 又会回调：

```objc
handler(nil)
```

这时候再进 `handler`，就会命中结束分支：

```objc
if (router == nil || [weakSelf _checkRecursiveBounds] == true) {
    ...
    completionHandler(weakSelf.currentRouter);
    ...
    weakSelf.isIdle = true;
    return;
}
```

这条 route 就结束了。

---

# 你可以把 `_routeHandle` 后面总结成一句话
**`_routeHandle` 之后，不是固定执行某个函数，而是取决于当前 router 里面有没有调用 `next(...)`。**

### 在你这个场景里通常是：
1. `_routeHandle(WBRouteManager)` 后
   执行 `next(actionManager)`

2. `_routeHandle(WBRouteActionManager)` 后
   执行 `handleRichMessages(...)`
   再 `next(nil)`

3. `handler(nil)` 收尾结束

---

# 最实用的断点顺序
你现在如果就在跟这条链，断点顺序应该盯这几个地方：

1. `handler(firstRouter)`
2. `_routeHandle(WBRouteManager, ...)`
3. [WBRouteManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteManager.m) 里 `name:@"action"` 的 block
4. `next(actionManager)`
5. `handler(actionManager)`
6. `_routeHandle(WBRouteActionManager, ...)`
7. [WBRouteActionManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteActionManager.m) 里 `name:@"popup"` 的 block
8. `handleRichMessages:`
9. `next(nil)`

---

# 如果你就想知道一句最直接的话
`_routeHandle` 之后，**正常应该进入当前 router 对应的 `handle` block，然后由那个 block 调 `next(...)`，继续推到下一个 router。**

如果你愿意，我可以下一条直接按你这个 case 画成：

`ar/event -> WBLinkActionModel -> WBRouteManager(action) -> WBRouteActionManager(popup) -> WBPopupManager`

这种一行一行的调用链。

# 如果你就想知道一句最直接的话
`_routeHandle` 之后，**正常应该进入当前 router 对应的 `handle` block，然后由那个 block 调 `next(...)`，继续推到下一个 router。**

如果你愿意，我可以下一条直接按你这个 case 画成：

`ar/event -> WBLinkActionModel -> WBRouteManager(action) -> WBRouteActionManager(popup) -> WBPopupManager`

这种一行一行的调用链。

payload.last_agent_message:
lelele，`_routeHandle` 之后，就看**当前这个 router 把 `next(...)` 往哪里传**。

你可以把它理解成：

- `deliver` 负责驱动
- `handler` 负责一跳一跳往前推
- `_routeHandle` 负责“当前这个 router 到底怎么处理这段路由”

---

## 你这条链的正常顺序是这样

假设 URL 是：

```objc
webox:///action/popup?data=...
```

### 1. `handler(firstRouter)`
这里的 `firstRouter` 是 `WBRouteManager`

### 2. `_routeHandle(WBRouteManager, ...)`
这一步会去执行 `WBRouteManager` 里注册的 route 规则，也就是  
[WBRouteManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteManager.m)

它里面有：

```objc
[route name:@"action" handle:^(NSDictionary * _Nullable userInfo, JUNRouterNextHandler  _Nonnull next) {
    WBRouteActionManager *actionManager = [WBRouteActionManager sharedManager];
    ...
    next(actionManager);
}];
```

### 3. `next(actionManager)`
这句就是 `_routeHandle` 之后最关键的动作。

它不会结束，而是会**再次调用外面的 `handler`**：

```objc
handler(actionManager)
```

---

## 所以 `_routeHandle` 之后，正常应该继续执行：
```objc
next(actionManager)
```

然后又回到：

```objc
handler(actionManager)
```

然后又会走：

```objc
[weakSelf _routeHandle:actionManager handler:handler queue:currentQueue];
```

---

## 到第二跳
这时候 current router 变成 `WBRouteActionManager`

然后 `_routeHandle(WBRouteActionManager, ...)` 会继续匹配下一段路由，比如：

- `popup`
- `exit`
- `vapp`

如果是 `popup`，就会命中  
[WBRouteActionManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteActionManager.m)

```objc
[route name:@"popup" handle:^(NSDictionary * _Nullable userInfo, JUNRouterNextHandler  _Nonnull next) {
    NSDictionary *richMessagesData = [userInfo[@"data"] jsonValueDecoded];
    ...
    [self handleRichMessages:richMessagesData];
    ...
    next(nil);
}];
```

---

## 所以第二个 `_routeHandle` 之后，正常应该执行：
```objc
[self handleRichMessages:richMessagesData];
next(nil);
```

---

## 然后 `next(nil)` 会怎样
`next(nil)` 又会回调：

```objc
handler(nil)
```

这时候再进 `handler`，就会命中结束分支：

```objc
if (router == nil || [weakSelf _checkRecursiveBounds] == true) {
    ...
    completionHandler(weakSelf.currentRouter);
    ...
    weakSelf.isIdle = true;
    return;
}
```

这条 route 就结束了。

---

# 你可以把 `_routeHandle` 后面总结成一句话
**`_routeHandle` 之后，不是固定执行某个函数，而是取决于当前 router 里面有没有调用 `next(...)`。**

### 在你这个场景里通常是：
1. `_routeHandle(WBRouteManager)` 后
   执行 `next(actionManager)`

2. `_routeHandle(WBRouteActionManager)` 后
   执行 `handleRichMessages(...)`
   再 `next(nil)`

3. `handler(nil)` 收尾结束

---

# 最实用的断点顺序
你现在如果就在跟这条链，断点顺序应该盯这几个地方：

1. `handler(firstRouter)`
2. `_routeHandle(WBRouteManager, ...)`
3. [WBRouteManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteManager.m) 里 `name:@"action"` 的 block
4. `next(actionManager)`
5. `handler(actionManager)`
6. `_routeHandle(WBRouteActionManager, ...)`
7. [WBRouteActionManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteActionManager.m) 里 `name:@"popup"` 的 block
8. `handleRichMessages:`
9. `next(nil)`

---

# 如果你就想知道一句最直接的话
`_routeHandle` 之后，**正常应该进入当前 router 对应的 `handle` block，然后由那个 block 调 `next(...)`，继续推到下一个 router。**

如果你愿意，我可以下一条直接按你这个 case 画成：

`ar/event -> WBLinkActionModel -> WBRouteManager(action) -> WBRouteActionManager(popup) -> WBPopupManager`

这种一行一行的调用链。

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.
