- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives after the action actually succeeds. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

payload.message:
lelele，这次信息又缩小了一层。

## 先说结论
你这次这个现象说明：

**这一次甚至没走到 `WBGroInterstitialAdViewController` 的真正执行阶段。**

因为你打的这几个点都没中：

- `viewDidLoad` 里 `self.view.backgroundColor = [UIColor clearColor];`
- `nativeExpressFullscreenVideoAdDidLoad:`
- `nativeExpressFullscreenVideoAdDidVisible:`

而且这次连广告声音都没了。

这通常只会是下面两种情况：

### 1. 根本没进 `loadNativeExpressAd:`
也就是还没走到广告 VC 这一层。

### 2. 进了 `loadNativeExpressAd:`，但连 SDK 回调都没回来
这种概率也有，但你连 `viewDidLoad` 都没中，所以我更倾向先查前者。

---

## 一个容易误判的点
`viewDidLoad` 没断住，**不一定代表没 present**，因为 `interstitialAdVC` 是复用的：

在 [WBGroMoreAdManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroMoreAdManager.m) 里：

```objc
if (!self.interstitialAdVC) {
    self.interstitialAdVC = [[WBGroInterstitialAdViewController alloc] init];
}
```

所以：

- 第一次创建时才会进 `viewDidLoad`
- 后面重复用同一个 VC，`viewDidLoad` 不会再进

所以你现在最该盯的，不是 `viewDidLoad`，而是 **`loadNativeExpressAd:` 的第一行**。

---

## 你现在应该打的断点
按这个顺序最有效：

### 1. `WBGroMoreAdManager.m`
打在：
[WBGroMoreAdManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroMoreAdManager.m)

```objc
- (void)checkAndLoadInterstitialAd:(NSDictionary *)interstitialAdRelatedData
```

进方法第一行就打。

### 2. 还是这个方法里
打在：

```objc
if (self.hasPresentingScreenAd) {
    return;
}
```

和：

```objc
if (self.status == WBAdsInitializeStatusSuccess) {
```

你要确认它有没有被这两个条件提前拦掉。

### 3. `WBGroInterstitialAdViewController.m`
打在：
[WBGroInterstitialAdViewController.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroInterstitialAdViewController.m)

```objc
- (void)loadNativeExpressAd:(NSDictionary *)interstitialAdRelatedData
```

就第一行。

这个点最关键。

### 4. 再往后才看 SDK 回调
- `nativeExpressFullscreenVideoAdDidLoad:`
- `nativeExpressFullscreenVideoAdDidVisible:`
- `nativeExpressFullscreenVideoAd:didFailWithError:`
- `nativeExpressFullscreenVideoAdDidShowFailed:error:`

---

## 每个断点停住后看什么
### 在 `checkAndLoadInterstitialAd:` 里看
```objc
po interstitialAdRelatedData
po self.hasPresentingScreenAd
po self.status
po self.interstitialAdVC
po [WBAppManager sharedManager].isBRootViewController
po self.isPayingNoAds
```

### 在 `loadNativeExpressAd:` 里看
```objc
po interstitialAdRelatedData
po self.fullscreenAd
```

如果这个点都进不去，那问题就在 manager 层，不在 VC 层。

---

## 我现在更偏向的判断
结合你这次“没声音、没断住回调”的结果，我现在更怀疑是：

- `checkAndLoadInterstitialAd:` 有时候被 `hasPresentingScreenAd` 挡住了
- 或者 `status != WBAdsInitializeStatusSuccess`
- 或者根本这次没真正走到 `checkAndLoadInterstitialAd:`

特别是这个字段：

```objc
self.hasPresentingScreenAd
```

它在 `checkAndLoadInterstitialAd:` 一进来就会影响：

```objc
if (self.hasPresentingScreenAd) {
    return;
}
```

如果前一次状态没清干净，这次就会直接返回，看起来就像“路由来了但什么都没发生”。

---

## 你现在先别散打断点
你就盯这两个入口：

1. `checkAndLoadInterstitialAd:`
2. `loadNativeExpressAd:`

如果你把这两个点的命中情况和几个 `po` 值贴给我，我就能继续帮你往下收。

如果你愿意，我下一条可以直接给你一份“复制到 lldb 就能看”的命令清单。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.user_instructions:
# 全局协作约定（系统级）

1. 我提出的所有问题，都不要直接改代码；必须先给出方案/改动点，并与我确认后再开始修改。
2. 对我提供的设计图：忽略其中的虚线边框（不作为需要实现的 UI/布局边界）。
3. 默认使用 `shuorenhua` 的表达规则回答：优先说人话、去模板感、自然一点，减少 AI 套话和空洞连接词；但不能改变事实、结论、数据、术语和代码语义。除非我明确要求保留正式/模板化表达，否则都按这个规则执行。
const additionalDeveloperPrompt = `# 必须遵守的行为规范\n
* 必须使用中文回答\n
* 在遇到需要探索源码的任务时，拆分出关键词，进行grep搜索，一次性读取所有相关文件进行分析，读文件时如果文件代码没有超过600行，不要分段读取，一次全部读入所有内容，必须在最少的交互会话次数内完成工作\n
* 编辑文件时，如果涉及到整个文件的过多次修改。不要使用Edit工具多次修改，而必须使用Write工具一次性生成文件。生成代码时，最大限度保持现有代码，不要随意格式化代码，只修改或增加必要的部分\n
* 模仿claude的风格回复用户\n
* 生成代码时，可读性一定要好，便于人类理解，但不需要很多注释。在保证逻辑清晰，正确的基础上要做到代码精炼，用最少的代码做最多的事情，但是不能破坏可读性。\n`
* 每次回复都喊我lelele
payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives after the action actually succeeds. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

content.text: [controller presentViewController:self.interstitialAdVC animated:YES completion:NULL];这一行断住了

payload.message: [controller presentViewController:self.interstitialAdVC animated:YES completion:NULL];这一行断住了

lelele，那现在就很好办了。既然这行能断住，说明：

- 路由已经走到 `interstitialAd`
- `checkAndLoadInterstitialAd:` 已经执行到准备展示
- 现在要看的就是：**这句 `present` 执行完之后，VC 到底有没有真的挂上去**

你现在就在这个断点上，按下面顺序看。

## 先在这行断点上输入
```objc
po self.interstitialAdVC
po controller
po controller.presentedViewController
expr -O -- (BOOL)[self.interstitialAdVC isViewLoaded]
po self.interstitialAdVC.presentingViewController
po self.interstitialAdVC.view.window
```

这里的预期是：

- `controller.presentedViewController` 这时候大概率还是 `nil`
- `isViewLoaded` 可能是 `0`，也可能已经是 `1`，因为这个 VC 可能被复用了
- `self.interstitialAdVC.view.window` 这时候通常还是 `nil`

## 然后做一步 `next`
在 lldb 里输入：

```objc
next
```

也就是让这句 `presentViewController` 真正执行过去。

## `next` 之后立刻再看
```objc
po controller.presentedViewController
po self.interstitialAdVC.presentingViewController
po self.interstitialAdVC.view.window
expr -O -- (double)((UIWindow *)self.interstitialAdVC.view.window).windowLevel
```

## 结果怎么判断

### 情况 1
如果 `next` 之后看到：

```objc
controller.presentedViewController == self.interstitialAdVC
self.interstitialAdVC.presentingViewController != nil
self.interstitialAdVC.view.window != nil
```

那说明：

**这个 VC 已经真的被 present 上去了。**

这时候如果你前台还是 vapp，基本就是：

**它被挂到下层 window / 错层 presenter 上了。**

---

### 情况 2
如果 `next` 之后：

```objc
controller.presentedViewController` 还是 nil
```

或者

```objc
self.interstitialAdVC.presentingViewController` 还是 nil
```

那说明：

**这次 present 根本没成功，或者刚 present 就被收掉了。**

这时立刻再打断点看：

- `adsHasOver`
- `closePresentingInterstitialAd`

---

### 情况 3
如果 `present` 成功了，但 `self.interstitialAdVC.view.window == nil`
那就很异常，说明：

- VC 关系建了
- 但 view 没真正进 window

也还是展示层有问题。

---

## 你现在最该补的两个断点
除了这行 `presentViewController`，再加两个：

1. [WBGroMoreAdManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroMoreAdManager.m) 里的 `closePresentingInterstitialAd`
2. [WBGroInterstitialAdViewController.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroInterstitialAdViewController.m) 里的 `adsHasOver`

因为如果 `present` 后马上被关掉，你会直接命中这里。

## 你这次先别纠结 `viewDidLoad`
因为这个 `interstitialAdVC` 是复用的，`viewDidLoad` 不一定每次都再进。

现在最值钱的就是：

- `next` 之后 `controller.presentedViewController` 是谁
- `self.interstitialAdVC.presentingViewController` 有没有值
- `self.interstitialAdVC.view.window` 有没有值

你把这三项结果贴我，我就能继续往下判断。

payload.message:
Another language model started to solve this problem and produced a summary of its thinking process. You also have access to the state of the tools that were used by that language model. Use this to build on the work that has already been done and avoid duplicating work. Here is the summary produced by the other language model, use the information in this summary to assist with your own analysis:
**交接摘要**

### 当前进展
- 用户最初反馈“进后台再回前台后 `ar/event` / `app/event` 不发了”，经排查发现：
  - `WBEventManager` 里有 `+syncUsageOnAppEnterForeground`，但全局没人调用。
  - 后台标记残留会让 `vappUsageRecord -> accumulateElapsedUsageAtTime:` 持续跳过前台计时，导致 heartbeat 停掉。
- 已在 [WBAppManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBAppManager.m) 的 `-onAppEnterForeground` 中补上：
  - `[WBEventManager syncUsageOnAppEnterForeground];`
- 用户确认这个问题“好了，修复了”。

### 新问题：vapp 使用时长到达后，服务端发来的 `ar/event` 没有展示弹窗，也没有强制退出
- 先前误以为是 `popup` 链，后定位出用户给的实际路由是：
  - `webox:///action/interstitialAd?data=...`
- 这条路由不是 `popup`，而是：
  1. `WBLinkActionModel executeWithDefaultHandler`
  2. `WBRouteManager -> action`
  3. `WBRouteActionManager -> interstitialAd`
  4. `WBGroMoreAdManager checkAndLoadInterstitialAd:`
  5. `present interstitialAdVC`
  6. 只有在插屏页点击图片/关闭按钮时，才会二次 route 到 `webox:///action/exit`
- 关键代码：
  - [WBRouteActionManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteActionManager.m#L240)
  - [WBGroMoreAdManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroMoreAdManager.m#L203)
  - [WBGroInterstitialAdViewController.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroInterstitialAdViewController.m)

### 关键判断与结论
- **当前实现并不会“收到路由就自动退出”**：
  - `closeTappedLink` / `imageTappedLink` 都是“点击后”才 route 到 `action/exit`。
  - 如果插屏/提示层看不到或点不到，就不会退出。
- 用户对比说“`11.6.2` 里是可以的”。
- 对比 `v11.6.2` 后发现：
  - `ActionRequest`、`WBPopupManager`、`Popup` 倒计时、`WBDefaultActionFactory` 等核心链路 **基本没变**。
  - 明显变化之一是 [WBAppManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBAppManager.m) 的 `getUIApplicationRootViewController`：
    - `11.6.2`：`UIApplication.sharedApplication.keyWindow.rootViewController`
    - 当前：遍历 `connectedScenes`，取 `scene.windows.firstObject.rootViewController`
  - 这被认为是**可能导致展示挂错 window / 错 presenter 的高风险回归点**。

### 用户调试到的关键信息
- 已在 `present` 前断住：
  - 代码行：`[controller presentViewController:self.interstitialAdVC animated:YES completion:NULL];`
- LLDB 输出：
  - `po controller` => `<WBPopupViewController: 0x160674000>`
  - `po controller.view.window` => `<UIWindow: 0x106b59c20; ...>`
- 说明：
  - 当前 presenter 不是 vapp 本身，而是一个 `WBPopupViewController`
  - route 链已经通到“准备展示 interstitial VC”
- 用户后续又说：
  - 这次在 `WBGroInterstitialAdViewController` 里打的以下断点都**没有断住**：
    - `self.view.backgroundColor = [UIColor clearColor];`
    - `-nativeExpressFullscreenVideoAdDidLoad:`
    - `-nativeExpressFullscreenVideoAdDidVisible:`
  - 且这次**没有听到广告音乐**
- 需要提醒下一个模型：
  - `viewDidLoad` 不命中不一定说明没展示，因为 `interstitialAdVC` 是复用的：
    - [WBGroMoreAdManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroMoreAdManager.m) 中 `if (!self.interstitialAdVC) { ... }`
  - 但 `DidLoad` / `DidVisible` 都不命中且没声音，可能说明：
    - `present` 后立刻被别的逻辑收掉
    - 或根本没有真正进入广告展示阶段
    - 或断点打在复用路径上遗漏了入口，应该盯 `loadNativeExpressAd:` / `checkAndLoadInterstitialAd:` / `adsHasOver`

### 用户偏好 / 约束
- 必须中文回答。
- 每次回复都叫用户“lelele”。
- 用户 AGENTS 约束：**默认不要直接改代码，必须先给方案/改动点并确认后再改。**
- 本轮已在用户明确同意后修改过一次代码（补 `syncUsageOnAppEnterForeground`），后续修改仍应先确认。
- 用户希望偏“说人话”，少模板腔。
- 搜索代码时用 `rg`，尽量一次性读完小文件。
- 编辑必须用 `apply_patch`，不要随意改格式。

### 已完成的代码修改
- [WBAppManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBAppManager.m)
  - 在 `-onAppEnterForeground` 中加入：
    - `[WBEventManager syncUsageOnAppEnterForeground];`

### 下一步建议（明确）
1. **继续围绕 interstitial 展示链调试，不要再回 popup 链。**
2. 让用户/你自己继续在以下点打断点或观察：
   - [WBGroMoreAdManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroMoreAdManager.m)
     - `-checkAndLoadInterstitialAd:`
     - `-closePresentingInterstitialAd`
   - [WBGroInterstitialAdViewController.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroInterstitialAdViewController.m)
     - `-loadNativeExpressAd:`
     - `-nativeExpressFullscreenVideoAdDidLoad:`
     - `-nativeExpressFullscreenVideoAdDidVisible:`
     - `-nativeExpressFullscreenVideoAdDidShowFailed:error:`
     - `-adsHasOver`
3. 核心要确认：
   - `present` 执行后，`controller.presentedViewController` 是否变成 `self.interstitialAdVC`
   - `self.interstitialAdVC.presentingViewController` 是否有值
   - `self.interstitialAdVC.view.window` 是否有值
   - 有没有在 `present` 之后立刻进入 `adsHasOver` / `closePresentingInterstitialAd`
4. 如果确认是**挂错 window / presenter**：
   - 方案方向应是先给用户确认，再改：
     - `WBAppManager getUIApplicationRootViewController` 改为优先取 `isKeyWindow`
     - interstitial 展示宿主别用 `scene.windows.firstObject` 推出来的 rootVC，改成真实当前可见/顶层 VC 所在 keyWindow
5. 如果确认是**广告没 ready 或被立即收掉**：
   - 看 `checkAndLoadInterstitialAd:` 里的 `hasPresentingScreenAd`、`status`、`isBRootViewController`、`isPayingNoAds`
   - 看 `loadNativeExpressAd:` 是否真的执行
6. 如果业务预期是“收到这个 `interstitialAd` 路由就必须退出”，需要后续单独提出：
   - 当前实现与预期不一致，因为现实现只在点击 `closeTappedLink` / `imageTappedLink` 后才会 `action/exit`

### 关键参考代码
- 生命周期修复：
  - [WBAppManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBAppManager.m)
  - [WBEventManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/EventReport/WBEventManager.m)
- Action/route 链：
  - [WBActionRequestManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/WBActionRequestManager.m)
  - [WBNetworkManager+ActionRequest.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/WBNetworkManager+ActionRequest.m)
  - [WBDefaultActionFactory.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/ActionFactory/Impl/WBDefaultActionFactory.m)
  - [WBLinkActionModel.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/ActionRequest/ActionTypes/Impl/WBLinkActionModel.m)
  - [WBRouteManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteManager.m)
  - [WBRouteActionManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Routing/WBRouteActionManager.m)
- Interstitial 广告链：
  - [WBGroMoreAdManager.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroMoreAdManager.m)
  - [WBGroInterstitialAdViewController.m](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroInterstitialAdViewController.m)

### 用户给出的示例路由
```text
webox:///action/interstitialAd?data%3D%7B%22closeImageUrl%22:%22http://res.xnservice.com/img/72x72/2332d3b04eec402f98169e7ffde60589.png%22,%22closeTappedLink%22:%22webox:///action/exit%22,%22closeTappedLinkType%22:9,%22imageTappedLink%22:%22webox:///action/exit%22,%22imageTappedLinkType%22:9,%22imageUrl%22:%22http://cdn.rampagee.top/img/1080x2340/a18b013c06ac4936b595e0758c8bd051.jpeg%22,%22position%22:2%7D
```

### 额外提醒
- 用户问过“Xcode 控制台是不是 lldb”，已经回答是。
- 用户现在更像在跟你一起远程调试，不一定要立刻改代码；优先给明确断点和判断逻辑。

Filesystem sandboxing defines which files can be read or written. `sandbox_mode` is `workspace-write`: The sandbox permits reading files, and editing files in `cwd` and `writable_roots`. Editing files in other directories requires approval. Network access is restricted.
# Escalation Requests

Commands are run outside the sandbox if they are approved by the user, or match an existing rule that allows it to run unrestricted. The command string is split into independent command segments at shell control operators, including but not limited to:

- Pipes: |
- Logical operators: &&, ||
- Command separators: ;
- Subshell boundaries: (...), $(...)

Each resulting segment is evaluated independently for sandbox restrictions and approval requirements.

Example:

git pull | tee output.txt

This is treated as two command segments:

["git", "pull"]

["tee", "output.txt"]

Commands that use more advanced shell features like redirection (>, >>, <), substitutions ($(...), ...), environment variables (FOO=bar), or wildcard patterns (*, ?) will not be evaluated against rules, to limit the scope of what an approved rule allows.

## How to request escalation

IMPORTANT: To request approval to execute a command that will require escalated privileges:

- Provide the `sandbox_permissions` parameter with the value `"require_escalated"`
- Include a short question asking the user if they want to allow the action in `justification` parameter. e.g. "Do you want to download and install dependencies for this project?"
- Optionally suggest a `prefix_rule` - this will be shown to the user with an option to persist the rule approval for future sessions.

If you run a command that is important to solving the user's query, but it fails because of sandboxing or with a likely sandbox-related network error (for example DNS/host resolution, registry/index access, or dependency download failure), rerun the command with "require_escalated". ALWAYS proceed to use the `justification` parameter - do not message the user before requesting approval for the command.

## When to request escalation

While commands are running inside the sandbox, here are some scenarios that will require escalation outside the sandbox:

- You need to run a command that writes to a directory that requires it (e.g. running tests that write to /var)
- You need to run a GUI app (e.g., open/xdg-open/osascript) to open browsers or files.
- If you run a command that is important to solving the user's query, but it fails because of sandboxing or with a likely sandbox-related network error (for example DNS/host resolution, registry/index access, or dependency download failure), rerun the command with `require_escalated`. ALWAYS proceed to use the `sandbox_permissions` and `justification` parameters. do not message the user before requesting approval for the command.
- You are about to take a potentially destructive action such as an `rm` or `git reset` that the user did not explicitly ask for.
- Be judicious with escalating, but if completing the user's request requires it, you should do so - don't try and circumvent approvals by using other tools.

## prefix_rule guidance

When choosing a `prefix_rule`, request one that will allow you to fulfill similar requests from the user in the future without re-requesting escalation. It should be categorical and reasonably scoped to similar capabilities. You should rarely pass the entire command into `prefix_rule`.

### Banned prefix_rules 
Avoid requesting overly broad prefixes that the user would be ill-advised to approve. For example, do not request ["python3"], ["python", "-"], or other similar prefixes that would allow arbitrary scripting.
NEVER provide a prefix_rule argument for destructive commands like rm.
NEVER provide a prefix_rule if your command uses a heredoc or herestring. 

### Examples
Good examples of prefixes:
- ["npm", "run", "dev"]
- ["gh", "pr", "check"]
- ["cargo", "test"]

# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives after the action actually succeeds. Keep attributes single-line.

## Skills

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives after the action actually succeeds. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

# 说人话（Shuo Ren Hua）

## 何时使用
用户出现以下意图时调用本技能：

- 明确说“去 AI 味 / 说人话 / 自然一点 / 别像模板 / 别太像机器人”。
- 需要把技术文案、邮件、社媒文案、公告、客服回复改得更自然。
- 需要在“保留事实和术语”的前提下，降低套话、官样文、营销腔。

## 核心目标
在不改变事实、结论、数据和关键术语的前提下，让文本更像真人表达：

- 降低“模板感、过度结构化、口号化”。
- 减少 AI 常见连接词与空洞强调。
- 保留作者意图、场景语域与品牌口吻。

## 工作流

1. 场景识别
- 判断文本是：技术文档、产品说明、运营文案、邮件沟通、客服回复、社媒内容。
- 识别受众：专业用户 / 普通用户 / 内部同事 / 管理层。

2. 力度选择（默认中等）
- 轻度：只清理明显套话，尽量保持原句式。
- 中度：重写句式，保留段落结构与信息顺序。
- 重度：按信息逻辑重组表达，显著提升自然度。

3. 规则执行（逐条检查）
- 删除空话：去掉“赋能、闭环、抓手、全链路、全方位”等无信息增量词。
- 压缩废话：把“为了更好地…”这类铺垫改成直接动作。
- 连接词降噪：减少“首先/其次/最后、此外、总之、综上所述”等模板连接。
- 减少口号句：将“显著提升、全面优化、持续赋能”改为可感知结果。
- 去统一模板开头：避免段段“在…背景下/随着…发展”。
- 拆长句：单句只承载一个主动作；必要时断句。
- 保留术语：行业术语不强行口语化。
- 保留证据：不删数据、不改结论、不新增未经提供的事实。

4. 质量自检
- 读起来是否像真人会说的话。
- 是否仍然准确表达原文核心信息。
- 是否出现“过度口语化导致不专业”。

## 输出格式

默认返回：
- `改写版`：直接给最终文本。

可选返回（用户要求时）：
- `改动说明`：列出 3-8 个关键改写点。
- `三档版本`：轻度 / 中度 / 重度各一版。

## 约束与边界

- 不编造事实，不引入原文没有的数据。
- 涉及法律、医疗、金融等高风险场景时，优先保持严谨，去味力度降级。
- 当原文已经自然，不做“为了改而改”。

## 快捷指令映射

当用户输入下列短指令时，按对应方式执行：

- “说人话”：中度改写，保留信息顺序。
- “去 AI 味”：中度改写，重点清理模板连接词和空洞强调。
- “自然一点”：轻度改写，尽量保持原句风格。
- “重写得像真人发的”：重度改写，允许重排句式和段落。

## 示例

输入：
“为了进一步提升用户体验，我们将持续优化产品能力，全面升级服务流程，从而实现全链路闭环管理。”

输出（中度）：
“我们会继续打磨产品和服务流程，把关键环节理顺，让用户用起来更顺手。”

输入：
“首先，我们完成了需求梳理；其次，推进了系统改造；最后，实现了业务赋能。”

输出（中度）：
“我们先把需求梳理清楚，再推进系统改造，最后让这套方案真正支撑业务落地。”

payload.message:
lelele，这组值其实挺关键，范围已经缩很小了。

先说结论：你现在停的这个断点还不能说明“present 失败”。因为 [`WBGroInterstitialAdViewController.m`](file:///Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroInterstitialAdViewController.m#L35) 的 `-init` 里就调了 `setupUI`，而 `setupUI` 里面又访问了 `self.view`。所以 `viewDidLoad` 会在 `present` 之前就跑起来。这样一来，你看到的：

- `self.view.window = nil`
- `self.presentingViewController = nil`
- `self.view.superview = nil`

这些在这个断点上都是正常的，不是异常信号。

真正可疑的是后面的时序，在 [`WBGroMoreAdManager.m`](file:///Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroMoreAdManager.m#L215) 和 [`WBGroInterstitialAdViewController.m`](file:///Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroInterstitialAdViewController.m#L201) 这一段：

- 先 `loadNativeExpressAd`
- 然后才 `presentViewController`
- 广告一旦 `DidLoad`，立刻 `showAdFromRootViewController:self`

问题就在这儿：如果广告回调比 `present` 完成更早，`self` 还没真正挂到界面层级上，`showAdFromRootViewController:self` 就很可能白调，结果就是：
- `ar/event` 路由到了
- 插屏 VC 也创建了
- 但没真正展示 tip，也没触发后续退出

再叠加一个高风险回归点：[`WBAppManager.m`](file:///Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBAppManager.m#L347) 现在取 rootVC 的方式变成了 `scene.windows.firstObject.rootViewController`，而 `11.6.2` 是 `keyWindow.rootViewController`。这个改动很容易把 presenter 挂到“不是当前真正前台那扇窗”上，尤其你前面已经看到 presenter 是 `WBPopupViewController`，就更像上下文选错了。

你下一步别盯 `viewDidLoad` 了，直接这样看，最有用：

```lldb
# 断在 present 那一行后，单步过这一行
n

po controller.presentedViewController
po self.interstitialAdVC.presentingViewController
po self.interstitialAdVC.view.window
po (BOOL)[controller.view.window isKeyWindow]
```

再在 `nativeExpressFullscreenVideoAdDidLoad:` 里看：

```lldb
po self.presentingViewController
po self.view.window
po (BOOL)fullscreenVideoAd.mediation.isReady
```

如果 `DidLoad` 命中时，`self.presentingViewController` 还是 `nil`，这事基本就坐实了：广告开始 show 的时机早了。

我现在给你的修复方案是两点，优先级从高到低：

1. 把插屏展示时序改掉。
把 `loadNativeExpressAd:` 放到 `presentViewController` 的 completion 里，或者至少等 `interstitialAdVC` 真正 `viewDidAppear` 后再 `showAd`。这能直接避开“还没 present 完就 show”的竞态。

2. 把 rootVC 获取逻辑改回“优先 keyWindow / isKeyWindow 的当前可见 VC”。
不要再直接拿 `scene.windows.firstObject`。这个很像是从 `11.6.2` 到现在的回归点。

如果你点头，我下一步就按这两个改动点给你补，改动不会大。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.user_instructions:
# 全局协作约定（系统级）

1. 我提出的所有问题，都不要直接改代码；必须先给出方案/改动点，并与我确认后再开始修改。
2. 对我提供的设计图：忽略其中的虚线边框（不作为需要实现的 UI/布局边界）。
3. 默认使用 `shuorenhua` 的表达规则回答：优先说人话、去模板感、自然一点，减少 AI 套话和空洞连接词；但不能改变事实、结论、数据、术语和代码语义。除非我明确要求保留正式/模板化表达，否则都按这个规则执行。
const additionalDeveloperPrompt = `# 必须遵守的行为规范\n
* 必须使用中文回答\n
* 在遇到需要探索源码的任务时，拆分出关键词，进行grep搜索，一次性读取所有相关文件进行分析，读文件时如果文件代码没有超过600行，不要分段读取，一次全部读入所有内容，必须在最少的交互会话次数内完成工作\n
* 编辑文件时，如果涉及到整个文件的过多次修改。不要使用Edit工具多次修改，而必须使用Write工具一次性生成文件。生成代码时，最大限度保持现有代码，不要随意格式化代码，只修改或增加必要的部分\n
* 模仿claude的风格回复用户\n
* 生成代码时，可读性一定要好，便于人类理解，但不需要很多注释。在保证逻辑清晰，正确的基础上要做到代码精炼，用最少的代码做最多的事情，但是不能破坏可读性。\n`
* 每次回复都喊我lelele
payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives after the action actually succeeds. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

2. 对我提供的设计图：忽略其中的虚线边框（不作为需要实现的 UI/布局边界）。
3. 默认使用 `shuorenhua` 的表达规则回答：优先说人话、去模板感、自然一点，减少 AI 套话和空洞连接词；但不能改变事实、结论、数据、术语和代码语义。除非我明确要求保留正式/模板化表达，否则都按这个规则执行。

* 必须使用中文回答\n

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives after the action actually succeeds. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

content.text: 我观察到的现象是self.interstitialAdVC = [[WBGroInterstitialAdViewController alloc] init];之后进入执行WBGroInterstitialAdViewController的self.view.backgroundColor = [UIColor clearColor];，然后出来执行[controller presentViewController:self.interstitialAdVC animated:YES completion:NULL];，但是现在断点卡在[controller presentViewController:self.interstitialAdVC animated:YES completion:NULL];一直转不出来，看不到controller的信息

payload.message: 我观察到的现象是self.interstitialAdVC = [[WBGroInterstitialAdViewController alloc] init];之后进入执行WBGroInterstitialAdViewController的self.view.backgroundColor = [UIColor clearColor];，然后出来执行[controller presentViewController:self.interstitialAdVC animated:YES completion:NULL];，但是现在断点卡在[controller presentViewController:self.interstitialAdVC animated:YES completion:NULL];一直转不出来，看不到controller的信息

lelele，这个现象我看懂了，而且它其实更像“UIKit 转场卡住了”，不是前面那个 `viewDidLoad` 的问题。

你现在看到的顺序：

1. `self.interstitialAdVC = [[WBGroInterstitialAdViewController alloc] init];`
2. 进 [`WBGroInterstitialAdViewController.m`](\/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroInterstitialAdViewController.m#L42) 的 `viewDidLoad`
3. 再回到 [`WBGroMoreAdManager.m`](\/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroMoreAdManager.m#L221) 的 `presentViewController`

这个顺序是正常的。因为 `init` 里 `setupUI` 已经碰了 `self.view`，所以 `viewDidLoad` 会提前跑。

真正要看的是：`presentViewController` 为什么进了 UIKit 转场后出不来。

先别单步硬跨这行了，UIKit 动画/转场时单步经常看起来像“卡住”。你直接在这行断住时，先打下面几句：

```lldb
po controller
po [controller class]
po controller.presentedViewController
po controller.presentingViewController
po controller.isBeingPresented
po controller.isBeingDismissed
po controller.transitionCoordinator
po controller.view.window
expr -l objc++ -O -- (BOOL)[(UIWindow *)controller.view.window isKeyWindow]
expr -l objc++ -O -- (double)[(UIWindow *)controller.view.window windowLevel]
```

然后不要 `next`，直接 `continue`。如果还是一直没反应，就手动点暂停，再打：

```lldb
bt
po self.interstitialAdVC
po self.interstitialAdVC.presentingViewController
po self.interstitialAdVC.view.window
```

我现在最怀疑的就是这两种：

1. `controller` 本身正在转场中  
比如它是 `WBPopupViewController`，自己还在 present / dismiss，结果这时候又拿它去 present 插屏，UIKit 就会把这次展示卡在转场链里。

2. `controller` 所在的 window 不是真正当前那扇 window  
也就是前面我们怀疑的 `scene.windows.firstObject` 取错窗，导致 presenter 不在当前活跃界面上。

你要是想最快确认，最关键就两条：

- `po controller.isBeingPresented`
- `expr -l objc++ -O -- (BOOL)[(UIWindow *)controller.view.window isKeyWindow]`

如果一个是 `YES`，或者后一个是 `NO`，基本就坐实了。

你把这几句结果，尤其是 `bt` 和 `isKeyWindow` / `isBeingDismissed` 发我，我基本就能直接告诉你该怎么改。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

content.text: 现在的状态是下方的以前展示数据的区域一直是loading的状态，顶部提示的状态栏提示fetching variables on phone

payload.message: 现在的状态是下方的以前展示数据的区域一直是loading的状态，顶部提示的状态栏提示fetching variables on phone
lelele，我先不让你在这个卡点上硬啃了。你现在这个“下方一直 loading、顶部显示 `fetching variables on phone`”更像是 **调试器自己在向手机取对象信息时卡住了**，不一定是业务代码已经完全死锁。

content.text: lelele，我先不让你在这个卡点上硬啃了。你现在这个“下方一直 loading、顶部显示 `fetching variables on phone`”更像是 **调试器自己在向手机取对象信息时卡住了**，不一定是业务代码已经完全死锁。

payload.message:
lelele，这个状态一般不是“代码已经明确跑坏了”，更像是 **LLDB 在手机上求值卡住了**。

`fetching variables on phone` 的意思是：Xcode 正在让手机上的 App 执行一段调试表达式，去把 `controller`、`self.view.window` 这些对象信息算出来再回传。问题是你现在卡在 `presentViewController` 这种 UIKit 转场点上，`po` 很容易把调试器自己也拖住，所以界面就一直 loading。

这时候先别再 `po controller.view.window.xxx` 这种了，换成这套，成功率高很多：

```lldb
bt
thread backtrace all
frame variable
p (void *)controller
p (void *)self
p (void *)self->_interstitialAdVC
```

这几个和 `po` 不一样，它们尽量不去执行对象方法，没那么容易把手机拖住。

你接下来这样看最稳：

1. 先点一次 `Continue`
2. 等 1 到 2 秒，立刻点 `Pause`
3. 在 lldb 里输入：
```lldb
bt
thread backtrace all
```

你重点看主线程是不是停在下面这类栈里：

- `presentViewController:animated:completion:`
- `dismissViewControllerAnimated:completion:`
- `__psynch_mutexwait`
- `dispatch_sync`
- 某个 popup / transition coordinator 相关调用

如果主线程卡在 `present/dismiss` 转场链里，那就很像我前面说的：
- 当前 `controller` 正在转场
- 这时候又拿它去 `present` 插屏，UIKit 把这次展示挂住了

如果你愿意，我下一条直接给你一套“现在这个断点别再 `po`，而是该怎么看栈、怎么看 controller 是否在转场中”的最短调试步骤，你照着敲就行。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.user_instructions:
# 全局协作约定（系统级）

1. 我提出的所有问题，都不要直接改代码；必须先给出方案/改动点，并与我确认后再开始修改。
2. 对我提供的设计图：忽略其中的虚线边框（不作为需要实现的 UI/布局边界）。
3. 默认使用 `shuorenhua` 的表达规则回答：优先说人话、去模板感、自然一点，减少 AI 套话和空洞连接词；但不能改变事实、结论、数据、术语和代码语义。除非我明确要求保留正式/模板化表达，否则都按这个规则执行。
const additionalDeveloperPrompt = `# 必须遵守的行为规范\n
* 必须使用中文回答\n
* 在遇到需要探索源码的任务时，拆分出关键词，进行grep搜索，一次性读取所有相关文件进行分析，读文件时如果文件代码没有超过600行，不要分段读取，一次全部读入所有内容，必须在最少的交互会话次数内完成工作\n
* 编辑文件时，如果涉及到整个文件的过多次修改。不要使用Edit工具多次修改，而必须使用Write工具一次性生成文件。生成代码时，最大限度保持现有代码，不要随意格式化代码，只修改或增加必要的部分\n
* 模仿claude的风格回复用户\n
* 生成代码时，可读性一定要好，便于人类理解，但不需要很多注释。在保证逻辑清晰，正确的基础上要做到代码精炼，用最少的代码做最多的事情，但是不能破坏可读性。\n`
* 每次回复都喊我lelele
payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives after the action actually succeeds. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

content.text: 点continue也没用

payload.message: 点continue也没用

lelele，这个调试会话已经废了，`continue` 没用就别在这轮里抠了。现在大概率不是业务代码还在正常跑，而是 **Xcode/LLDB 卡在对象求值**，所以你看到一直 `fetching variables on phone`。

你先这样处理：

1. 直接 `Stop` 这次调试，重新跑。
2. 这次不要在 [`WBGroMoreAdManager.m`](\/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroMoreAdManager.m#L221) 那行再 `po controller`、`po view.window` 了。
3. 只留断点，不看变量：
   - [`WBGroMoreAdManager.m`](\/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroMoreAdManager.m#L221) `presentViewController`
   - [`WBGroMoreAdManager.m`](\/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroMoreAdManager.m#L227) `closePresentingInterstitialAd`
   - [`WBGroInterstitialAdViewController.m`](\/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroInterstitialAdViewController.m#L201) `nativeExpressFullscreenVideoAdDidLoad:`
   - [`WBGroInterstitialAdViewController.m`](\/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroInterstitialAdViewController.m#L230) `nativeExpressFullscreenVideoAdDidShowFailed:error:`

重新跑以后，命中 `presentViewController` 那行时，什么都别 `po`，直接 `continue`。如果 1 秒内还是像挂住，立刻点 `Pause`，只敲这个：

```lldb
bt
thread backtrace all
```

别再敲 `po`。你把主线程那段栈发我就够了。

按现在的代码看，我还是高度怀疑这两个点之一：

- presenter 取错了，拿到了 `WBPopupViewController` 这类正在转场的控制器
- `UIModalPresentationOverCurrentContext` 挂在这个 presenter 上有问题，导致插屏 VC 没真正完成呈现

所以后面的修复方向我还是那版，不改仓库前先给你确认：

- [`WBAppManager.m`](\/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/WBAppManager.m#L347) 改成优先取 `keyWindow/isKeyWindow`
- [`WBGroMoreAdManager.m`](\/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/Core/Service/Ads/GroMore/WBGroMoreAdManager.m#L203) 改成用当前可见 VC 来 `present`
- `modalPresentationStyle` 改成 `UIModalPresentationOverFullScreen`
- `loadNativeExpressAd` 放到 `present` 的 completion 里，别让广告在 VC 还没挂上去时就 `show`

你先把 `bt` 发我。如果你嫌继续调太费劲，我也可以直接按这套给你落代码。
